title: Koellia virginica
taxonomy: Koellia virginica is classified within the family of plants known as Caryophyllaceae and belongs to the order Caryophyllales. This classification places it within the subclass Rosidae and the class Magnoliopsida. The class Magnoliopsida is a part of the division Magnoliophyta. It is further classified into the genus Koellia, a group of plants that are native to North America. Koellia virginica is a species within this genus that is native to the United States.

intro: Koellia virginica, also known as Koellia, is a flowering plant species in the family Gentianaceae. Koellia virginica has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which may help in the management of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Koellia virginica include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Koellia virginica is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
